body.note: Sequentibus Coss. For at Rome they reckoned the yeares according to their Consuls: whose office ordinarily continued one yeare, and began with the yeare, upon the first day of Januarie.
Or Divorse.
Of Marius.
To flie into the Sabines Countrie.
For Aurelia was his mother.
M. Minutius Thermus.
Surnamed so of the people in Cilicia named Isauri, whom he subdued.
So variable and indiscreet.
Whiles hee governed his Province.
For calling into question so honorable a person.
Moloni, not Molonis, as Plutarch taketh it, that is, the son of Molon.
Hibernis mensibus, that is, In the Winter moneths Decemb. Januar. Febr.
Some reade dignatione in a divers sense.
Medico, vel amico, that is, a friend.
Or the rest of his companions and servants.
To the Cities of Asia, a Province adjoyning.
C. Cotta, M. Crassus, and Cn. Pompeius, who were the chiefe.
A Tribune of the Commons.
Lepidus.
Treasurer.
Of the Goddesse Bona: which were celebrated in Cæsars house, being the Pontifex.
Called Betica.
Antistius Vetus.
In head shire Townes which were called Conventus. Plin. lib. 3. cap. 1. That is, 33. C. Philip. 5.
That had been Consul.
Or L. rather.
So called of a river, neere into which they dwelt beyond the Po.
Who was slaine by Spanish Horsemen, of whom hee had the conduct.
Geminis fratribus, that is, Castor and Pollux, who commonly be called Gemini fratres.
And yet hee exhibited 320 paire, as Plutarch writeth.
That hee might governe it and place the King againe in his roiall Seate.
Ptolomeus Auletes the Father of Cleopatra, who many yeares after by Gabinius was restored to his Kingdome.
As Torrentius saith.

This is by the figure Prolepsis to be understood of Cæsar when hee was Prætour of the Citie: as who favoured the Faction of Marius both then and before, howsoever it may seem that Suetonius speaketh this of him being Ædile, or presently after his Ædileship: which by Torrentius leave, may well stand with the truth.
In place of the Pretor.
Cæsar.
That is, Egipt and the restoring of the king afore said.
Which were 35.
But not entred yet into the Office.
Ultimum supplicium.
As if he ment by ultimum supplicium, imprisonment or some lesse punishment then death.
Quintus Cicero.
M. Cicero.
Of Consul and Senate.
Plutarch nameth Curio for one of them.
Of M. T. Cicero the Consul his yeere which now drew to an end.
That is, to Cn. Pompeius.
Surnamed Nepos (as Valerius witnesseth) for his riotous life and behaviour.
Indice, some reade Judice, that is, as if Judex were his surname.
Ex prætura, whereby it appeareth he was Prator Urbanus.
Cato, and his followers.
Optimates.
Consull.
Turbis alias culpis, that is, Trespasses or offences.
Obnunciaret, by pronouncing out of the Augurs learning, that the day was nefastus et non comitialis, that is, no Law-day.
Per edicta, some read, per lictores, that is, by his Serjeants and Officers.
At the discretion of xx. men deputed Commissioners for that purpose.
For that they had taken things at too high a rate.
Three a clock in the after-noone.
Indicem, others read Judicem, that is Vettius Judex.
Id est Vettius Judex aforesaid: For, dead hee was found in prison by night.
Whom hee promised in mariage the daughter of Cn. Pompeius.
Piso.
Cn. Pompeius.
Whether they should be repealed or stand in force.
When he was Consul.
For that hee was extraordinarily absent, longer than the Law Sempronia did permit.
The bird Galerita or Cassita, so called of a crest, upon the head. This Legion it should seeme ware Plumes of feathers in their crests of Helmets, whereupon it tooke that name.
Namely Cato, Plutarch.
Aurelia a Dame of singular chastitie.
Neptem, alii nepotem, that is, Nephew.
By Milo.
That is, a hundred millians of Sesterces, and 20, as Plinie writeth, lib. 36, cap. 15, if Glareanus readeth truly, Millies ducenties.
Domesticatim.
So, hee was great Unkle unto her, like as he was to Octavius Augustus, the Emperour.
Or Patrone.
Ei plebiscito.
